canonical_question: What are the Salesforce Composite API and Composite Graph API subrequest limits and capabilities?
aliases: sObject Collections vs Composite Batch vs sObject Tree API
Salesforce composite API reference ID between subrequests

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: v66.0 (Spring '26) — legacy hostname redirects removed; Connected App creation disabled by default

constraints: Composite API: max 25 subrequests per call; max 5 can be query or sObject Collections operations
Composite Graph: max 500 subrequests per graph; max 500 total across all graphs in a single request
sObject Collections: max 200 records per request (create, update, delete, upsert)
sObject Tree: max 200 total records across all trees; max 5 levels deep; max 5 sObject types per request
Composite Batch: max 25 subrequests; no reference IDs between subrequests (independent execution)
Entire composite request counts as 1 API call against org daily limit, but governor limits apply cumulatively across all subrequests
API versions 21.0-30.0 retired June 2025 — minimum supported version is v31.0

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs to process >10,000 records in batch
User needs to understand per-transaction governor limits in depth
User needs API versioning and deprecation strategy

alternative_to.label: Bulk API 2.0 — alternative for high-volume operations

often_confused_with.label: Bulk API is async/CSV-based; Composite is sync/JSON-based

inputs_needed.question: What integration pattern do you need?
